greenhouse._dropped_2026_05_31: Removed SF/NY-onsite-first tech companies: anthropic, notion, vercel, linear, replit, huggingface, figma, airtable, discord, stripe, mercury, ramp, wise, mongodb, cloudflare, glean, duolingo, perplexity, openphone, datadog, includedhealth. Their boards dump ~70% Senior/Sales roles Carlos can't apply to, burning LLM tokens on auto-closes. Kept: remote-first generalists + healthcare/RCM + civic-tech mission orgs.

jobspy._disabled_sites_note: indeed re-enabled 2026-06-20 (verified working, ~66% of all application volume). glassdoor=HTTP 400 'location not parsed' (Glassdoor tightened API). zip_recruiter=HTTP 403 Cloudflare bot-block. google=hangs the run when results_wanted > 30 (rate-limit, no timeout). Re-enable those only if JobSpy ships a fix or we add per-site timeout.
jobspy._location_note: Narrowed United States -> Philadelphia 2026-06-23. JobSpy now covers LOCAL (<=30 min); national REMOTE comes from the remote feeds + indeed-apify(locations:[remote]). Stops jobspy scraping the whole country.

usajobs._location_note: Narrowed United States -> Philadelphia 2026-06-23. Was pulling ALL federal jobs nationwide with blank parsed locations = ~55% of the queue. Philly-area federal only now.

_disabled_pending_playwright._note: Idealist + NeoGov + Dice all use client-side rendering for their job lists. Building scrapers requires Playwright (browser automation). Wired into config schema but disabled until Playwright is added.

location_policy._metro_note: Whole-state 'pennsylvania'/'pa' REMOVED 2026-06-23 — a bare PA token let Pittsburgh/Lancaster (5 hrs away) read as Philly-metro. The hard ingest gate (ingest_location_allowed) uses a curated <=30-min town list instead; this short list only feeds the scoring multiplier.

matching._lowered_2026_06_16: Lowered from 0.26 to 0.10 (combined) and 0.4 to 0.20 (pass-verdict). Mass-apply mode needs high recall not high precision. Bartlett (0.158) and other real fits were silently filtered. Carlos filters at the UI.
matching._opened_2026_06_20: Score gate set to 0.0 (was 0.10/0.20). It was destroying 836 of 1089 closes (76%) — jobs Carlos never saw. The UI min-rank slider already filters by score NON-destructively, so the auto-close was redundant AND lossy. Now only location (onsite/wrong-state) + junk close jobs; everything else reaches the queue and Carlos filters with the slider + lane tabs.
